strand: Exploring materials, media and techniques
gapNote: No app lets young children explore colour mixing, textures, tools or simple making.
title: Magic Mixing Pots
concept: Tap two paint pots and watch them swirl into a new colour live, with a wheel that predicts the result before children try it on the easel.

strand: Being imaginative and expressive through art and design
gapNote: Nothing supports child-initiated creating, representing ideas or talking about own work.
title: My Picture Story
concept: Children build a scene from stampable shapes and brushes, then the teacher taps each part so the child narrates what it is, building art-talk vocabulary.

strand: Colour, shape and pattern recognition
gapNote: No early art app teaches naming colours, simple shapes and repeating patterns.
title: Pattern Train
concept: Drag coloured carriages to continue a repeating AB/ABB pattern; the train chuffs along correctly only when the pattern holds.

strand: Responding to and talking about art
gapNote: No app prompts children to share feelings and observations about images and artists.
title: How Does It Make You Feel?
concept: Project a famous painting and let the class tap emotion faces and colour swatches that match the mood, sparking whole-carpet discussion.

strand: Drawing, painting and developing techniques (line, shape, form, space)
gapNote: No app teaches the visual elements or lets pupils practise line, shape and form.
title: Line Lab
concept: Pupils drag to make wavy, zigzag, thick and thin lines, then match each line type to a feeling and use it to draw a creature live.

strand: Colour theory and colour mixing
gapNote: Primary/secondary colour mixing is not covered by any art app.
title: Colour Mixer Easel
concept: Drag primaries together to make secondaries on an interactive palette, with a colour wheel that fills in as the class discovers each new colour.

strand: Using materials creatively (collage, printmaking, sculpture, textiles)
gapNote: No app supports designing and making with a range of materials and techniques.
title: Print Press
concept: Carve a simple stamp, ink it and press repeats to build a pattern, demonstrating how printmaking makes identical marks.

strand: Imagination, observation and recording ideas (sketchbooks)
gapNote: Nothing models recording observations or using a sketchbook to develop ideas.
title: Observation Station
concept: A photo fades to faint outline so pupils trace what they truly see, building looking-and-drawing habits the class can compare.

strand: Knowledge of artists, craftspeople and designers
gapNote: No app introduces named artists or links pupils' work to artists' techniques.
title: Artist Detective
concept: Reveal an artist's painting piece by piece while the class guesses the subject, then a one-tap fact card explains the artist and technique.

strand: Mastering drawing techniques (line, tone, perspective, proportion)
gapNote: No app builds the increasing mastery of drawing expected at KS2.
title: Vanishing Point
concept: Drag a horizon line and vanishing point to watch a street snap into one-point perspective, teaching depth interactively.

strand: Colour, pattern, texture and the visual elements
gapNote: Colour relationships and visual-element vocabulary are not addressed.
title: Colour Harmony Wheel
concept: Spin the wheel to find complementary, analogous and warm/cool sets, previewing each scheme on a sample artwork live.

strand: Painting, printmaking, sculpture and textiles techniques
gapNote: No app demonstrates the range of making techniques and materials.
title: Clay Studio 3D
concept: Pinch, coil and slab a virtual pot on the board, rotating it 360 degrees to show how 3D form is built up.

strand: Developing ideas and reviewing work in sketchbooks
gapNote: Nothing supports planning, refining and self-evaluating a piece of work.
title: Idea Web Studio
concept: Build a digital mood board from a theme word, then drag the strongest ideas into a development sequence the class critiques together.

strand: Great artists, architects and designers in history
gapNote: No app teaches art movements or the work of significant artists and designers.
title: Movement Timeline
concept: Scrub a timeline from Renaissance to Pop Art; each stop morphs a sample image into that style so pupils see the movements change.

strand: Evaluating and analysing creative works (art critique)
gapNote: Structured looking at and discussing artworks is not supported.
title: Critique Lens
concept: Annotate a projected artwork with composition lines, colour callouts and mood tags using a guided describe-analyse-interpret-judge frame.

strand: Drawing and recording from observation, experience and imagination
gapNote: No app develops the observational drawing and recording expected at KS3.
title: Sight-Size Drawing
concept: Overlay a measuring grid and plumb lines on a still-life photo so pupils learn to check angles and proportion as they draw.

strand: Mastery of media, materials and techniques (2D and 3D)
gapNote: Increasing proficiency across a wide range of media is not addressed.
title: Media Mixer
concept: Switch a single drawing between charcoal, ink wash, lino print and collage to compare how each medium changes mark and mood.

strand: Formal elements and composition (line, tone, colour, form, space)
gapNote: Composition and the formal elements as a deliberate design tool are uncovered.
title: Composition Grid
concept: Drag focal points onto rule-of-thirds and golden-ratio overlays to see how placement changes the balance of a photo or painting.

strand: Developing ideas through investigation: sketchbooks and the design process
gapNote: No app models artist research, idea development and personal response.
title: Project Journey
concept: Walk a class through a real GCSE-style project flow from artist research to final piece, dragging evidence into each assessment objective.

strand: History and contexts of art, craft and design (movements and cultures)
gapNote: Art movements, periods and cultural contexts are not taught by any app.
title: Style Decoder
concept: Upload or pick an image and the app highlights the visual cues that place it in Cubism, Impressionism, Surrealism and more, with movement fact cards.

strand: Critical analysis and evaluation of own and others' work
gapNote: Structured critique and self-evaluation against criteria is not supported.
title: Gallery Critique
concept: Anonymous peer work is projected and the class tags strengths and next steps against the assessment objectives in a live gallery walk.

strand: Developing ideas through investigations and artist research (AO1)
gapNote: No app supports the GCSE AO1 research-and-development requirement.
title: AO1 Research Builder
concept: Scaffold a contextual study page: drag artist images, annotate influences and link findings to the pupil's own intentions.

strand: Refining work by exploring media, materials and techniques (AO2)
gapNote: Media experimentation and refinement for AO2 is not addressed.
title: Experiment Lab
concept: Run side-by-side media trials on one motif and tag which experiments to take forward, modelling AO2 refinement decisions.

strand: Recording ideas, observations and insights (AO3)
gapNote: Observational recording and reflective annotation for AO3 is uncovered.
title: Observation Logbook
concept: Timed observational drawing challenges paired with prompted annotations that meet AO3 recording expectations.

strand: Presenting a personal and meaningful response: the final piece (AO4)
gapNote: Planning and realising a coherent final outcome for AO4 is not supported.
title: Final Piece Planner
concept: Assemble compositional studies into a mock-up of the final piece and check it connects back to AO1-AO3 evidence.

strand: Formal elements, composition and visual language at depth
gapNote: Advanced use of the formal elements is not taught by any app.
title: Visual Language Studio
concept: Manipulate colour, tone, scale and texture on a sample work and instantly see how meaning and mood shift.

strand: Critical and contextual understanding (movements, cultures, careers)
gapNote: No app builds the contextual knowledge underpinning GCSE Art and Design.
title: Context Connector
concept: Map an artwork to its movement, social context and a modern creative career, showing why context matters in coursework.

strand: Independent project development and sustained enquiry
gapNote: No app scaffolds the self-directed, sustained enquiry central to A-level.
title: Enquiry Tracker
concept: Plot a personal investigation as a living map of questions, experiments and decisions students update across the project.

strand: Advanced media, materials and technical mastery
gapNote: Sophisticated, controlled use of media and processes is not supported.
title: Technique Masterclass
concept: Step-through demos of advanced processes (glazing, etching, mixed media) with parameter sliders to see each variable's effect.

strand: Critical, contextual and theoretical analysis
gapNote: Deep critical analysis and the related study/personal study essay are uncovered.
title: Personal Study Workbench
concept: Build an evidenced visual essay, linking analysis, quotation and the student's practice with structure prompts for the related study.

strand: Personal investigation: synthesising ideas into resolved outcomes
gapNote: Synthesis of research into resolved personal outcomes is not modelled.
title: Synthesis Studio
concept: Cluster research strands and pull threads together into a planned resolved outcome, exposing the synthesis examiners reward.

strand: Connections between practice, contexts and contemporary art
gapNote: Situating personal work within contemporary and historical practice is uncovered.
title: Contemporary Compass
concept: Position a student's work on axes of medium, concept and era against living artists to find genuine, defensible connections.

strand: Singing songs, rhymes and chants
gapNote: No app supports learning, joining in with or performing nursery rhymes and simple songs.
title: Sing-Along Story Time
concept: Animated nursery-rhyme player with bouncing-ball lyric highlighting and a record-yourself-singing button the class can play back together.

strand: Exploring sounds and instruments (timbre)
gapNote: Nothing lets children explore and match the sounds of percussion instruments and everyday objects.
title: What Made That Sound?
concept: Tap a hidden instrument, hear it, then drag the matching picture; reveals shaker, drum, bells, triangle with big projectable visuals.

strand: Keeping a steady beat and moving to music
gapNote: No app builds pulse awareness through clapping, tapping and movement.
title: March, Tiptoe, Stomp
concept: Music changes tempo and the on-screen character marches, tiptoes or stomps; class copies the movement to feel fast and slow.

strand: Listening and responding to music
gapNote: Nothing prompts children to listen attentively and respond to mood and contrast in short pieces.
title: How Does It Feel?
concept: Plays a short musical clip then asks children to tap a happy, sleepy, spooky or busy face, opening simple talk about musical mood.

strand: Making and creating music (early composing)
gapNote: No app lets children invent simple sound patterns or sequences.
title: Sound Story Maker
concept: Drag weather, animal and instrument sounds onto a timeline to build a sound story for a picture book the teacher reads aloud.

strand: Singing and using the voice expressively
gapNote: No app supports tunefully singing songs, chants and rhymes or vocal expression.
title: Pitch Caterpillar
concept: Children sing into the mic and a caterpillar climbs higher for higher notes, giving instant visual feedback on matching pitch.

strand: Playing tuned and untuned instruments
gapNote: Nothing lets pupils play simple rhythmic and melodic parts on classroom instruments.
title: Glockenspiel Hero
concept: On-screen glockenspiel with colour-coded bars; scrolling colour cues let the whole class play a simple melody together.

strand: Exploring the dimensions of music (pitch, duration, dynamics, tempo, timbre)
gapNote: No app teaches high/low, long/short, loud/quiet, fast/slow or instrument sounds interactively.
title: High Low Slider
concept: Drag a balloon up and down and the pitch rises and falls, so children hear and see the link between visual height and pitch.

strand: Listening with concentration to a range of music
gapNote: Nothing structures attentive listening to live and recorded music across styles.
title: Listening Detectives
concept: Plays a clip and pupils tap when they spot an instrument or a change in speed; a scoreboard celebrates careful listeners.

strand: Experimenting, creating and combining sounds (composing)
gapNote: No app supports inventing and combining sounds to create simple compositions.
title: Sound Soup
concept: Drag sounds into a pot and stir to combine them, then play back the mix to explore texture and layering.

strand: Introducing simple notation and symbols
gapNote: Nothing introduces graphic scores or simple symbols to represent sounds.
title: Draw Your Music
concept: Children draw lines and shapes that the app turns into sound, building the idea that symbols can represent music.

strand: Singing and performing in solo and ensemble contexts
gapNote: No app supports singing in unison and parts or performing with confidence and control.
title: Round Robin Rounds
concept: Teaches singing rounds: the app holds each group's part with a moving cursor so two and three-part rounds stay together.

strand: Playing instruments with increasing accuracy and control
gapNote: Nothing supports developing technique on tuned/untuned instruments or whole-class instrumental teaching.
title: Recorder Trainer
concept: Fingering charts plus a tuner that listens and confirms each note, scaffolding whole-class recorder or wind teaching.

strand: Composing and improvising music
gapNote: No app lets pupils improvise and compose for a range of purposes using the dimensions of music.
title: Pentatonic Improviser
concept: A five-note pentatonic pad over a backing groove so any combination sounds good, freeing pupils to improvise confidently.

strand: Listening, appraising and developing understanding (dimensions of music)
gapNote: Nothing develops analytical listening using texture, structure, tempo, dynamics and timbre vocabulary.
title: Texture Layers Explorer
concept: Strip out and add back instrumental layers of a real track so the class hears and labels thin versus thick texture.

strand: Reading and using staff and other musical notations
gapNote: No app teaches reading staff notation, note names, rhythms and musical symbols.
title: Stave Climber
concept: Notes appear on the treble stave and pupils name them or tap the matching keyboard key, levelling up through note ranges.

strand: History of music and great composers and musicians
gapNote: Nothing introduces the history of music across periods, traditions and great composers.
title: Music Through Time
concept: An interactive timeline from Baroque to today; tap an era to hear a defining clip and meet a key composer or musician.

strand: Performing: singing and playing with technical control and expression
gapNote: No app supports playing and performing fluently on instruments and voice with control and expression.
title: Keyboard Skills Studio
concept: On-screen keyboard with chord-shape overlays and scrolling notation so pupils build performance pieces hands-together.

strand: Composing and improvising across styles and genres
gapNote: Nothing supports composing using staves and technologies, drawing on a range of musical structures and styles.
title: DAW Lite Composer
concept: A simplified multitrack sequencer to layer drums, bass, chords and melody, teaching arranging and the elements of composition.

strand: Listening, analysing and appraising music with understanding
gapNote: No app develops critical listening and analysis using the elements of music and technical vocabulary.
title: Active Listening Annotator
concept: A waveform with tap-to-tag markers for tempo, dynamic and instrumentation changes, building analytical listening skills.

strand: Music theory, notation and the elements of music
gapNote: Nothing teaches staff notation, keys, scales, intervals, chords and rhythm theory at KS3 depth.
title: Theory Trainer
concept: Adaptive drills on note reading, key signatures, scales and intervals with a built-in keyboard for instant aural checking.

strand: Music technology, recording and production
gapNote: No app introduces sequencing, sampling, recording and production used in modern music-making.
title: Beat Maker Sequencer
concept: A step sequencer for programming drum patterns and basslines, teaching how electronic and pop grooves are built.

strand: Music history, traditions and world musics
gapNote: Nothing covers the development of music across periods, cultures and global traditions.
title: World Music Atlas
concept: A clickable world map that plays gamelan, samba, raga and West African drumming with the features that define each tradition.

strand: Performing (solo and ensemble) for assessment
gapNote: No app supports the GCSE performance component, technique, accuracy and expression under recording conditions.
title: Performance Logbook
concept: Record performances against a click, self-mark on accuracy, fluency and expression using the GCSE descriptors, and track progress.

strand: Composing to a brief and free composition
gapNote: Nothing scaffolds the GCSE composition coursework: briefs, development, structure and notated/produced submissions.
title: Composition Brief Builder
concept: Generates GCSE-style briefs then guides through melody, harmony, structure and development with audio playback at each step.

strand: Listening and appraising set works and unfamiliar music
gapNote: No app trains the listening exam: analysing set works and unfamiliar extracts across areas of study.
title: Set Work Dissector
concept: Synced score-and-audio playback that highlights key features of GCSE set works for analysis and revision.

strand: Areas of study and musical contexts (genres, styles, traditions)
gapNote: Nothing covers the GCSE areas of study spanning Western classical, popular, fusion and world music contexts.
title: Areas of Study Explorer
concept: Interactive breakdown of each GCSE area of study with defining features, key works and listening examples.

strand: Music theory, harmony and notation for GCSE
gapNote: No app consolidates the theory, harmony, rhythm and notation underpinning all three GCSE components.
title: GCSE Theory Bootcamp
concept: Targeted drills on keys, scales, chords, intervals and rhythmic dictation with instant aural confirmation.

strand: Advanced performing (recital and ensemble)
gapNote: No app supports A-level recital-standard performance preparation, interpretation and recording.
title: Recital Prep Studio
concept: Programme planning, take recording against a click and self-assessment on interpretation, stylistic awareness and technical command.

strand: Advanced composition and arranging
gapNote: Nothing scaffolds free and brief-based A-level composition, including stylistic pastiche and orchestration.
title: Pastiche Composer
concept: Compose in Bach chorale or other set styles with rule-checking that flags parallel fifths and weak voice-leading in real time.

strand: Listening, analysis and appraising set works
gapNote: No app supports in-depth A-level analysis of prescribed works and dictation under exam conditions.
title: Analytical Score Annotator
concept: Synced score and audio with harmonic, structural and motivic annotation tools for deep set-work study.

strand: Music history, style and contextual study
gapNote: Nothing covers the historical, stylistic and cultural context required for A-level areas of study.
title: Style Period Deep Dive
concept: Interactive study of each prescribed area's history, conventions and influential figures with comparative listening.

strand: Advanced harmony, counterpoint and theory
gapNote: No app develops the advanced harmony, counterpoint and analytical theory expected at A-level.
title: Harmony and Counterpoint Lab
concept: Realise figured bass and write species counterpoint with live audio and automated checking of voice-leading rules.

strand: Music technology: recording, sequencing and production (Music Tech route)
gapNote: Nothing covers the A-level Music Technology skills of recording, sequencing, mixing and production techniques.
title: Mixdown Mastermind
concept: Interactive mixing desk where students balance, EQ and apply effects to stems, learning production decisions by ear.

strand: Technology in the everyday world
gapNote: No app helps young children recognise and talk about everyday technology (tills, cameras, traffic lights, phones).
title: Spot the Technology
concept: Tap-to-reveal scene of a home and high street; children find devices that use technology and hear what each one does.

strand: Early control and cause-and-effect (programmable toys)
gapNote: Nothing models giving simple instructions to a Bee-Bot style toy or button-press cause and effect.
title: Bee-Bot Floor Path
concept: Drag forward/turn arrows onto a giant floor mat, press Go and watch the bee buzz the route; class predicts where it lands.

strand: Following and giving simple instructions (early algorithms)
gapNote: No play-based sequencing activity (first/next/then) to build precise instruction-giving.
title: Make a Jam Sandwich Robot
concept: Children drag step cards in order; a literal robot follows them exactly, getting it hilariously wrong if a step is missing.

strand: Staying safe with devices (early online safety)
gapNote: No age-appropriate prompt on safe screen use and telling a trusted adult.
title: Ask a Grown-Up
concept: Friendly characters meet pop-ups and unknown buttons; class votes whether to tap or fetch a trusted adult.

strand: Algorithms: understanding precise step-by-step instructions
gapNote: No app teaching that algorithms are unambiguous ordered instructions, and predicting their behaviour.
title: Algorithm Detective
concept: Class debugs a wonky set of get-dressed instructions, reordering steps until the on-screen character is correctly dressed.

strand: Creating and debugging simple programs
gapNote: No block-coding sandbox for writing and fixing simple programs at KS1 level.
title: ScratchJr Stage
concept: Drag picture-blocks to make a sprite move and speak; deliberate bugs are seeded so the class hunts and fixes them together.

strand: Logical reasoning and prediction (sequence and outcome)
gapNote: Nothing asks pupils to predict the behaviour of a given simple program before running it.
title: Predict the Path
concept: Show a short command sequence for a robot on a grid; pupils vote on the end square, then reveal the run.

strand: Using technology purposefully (create, store, retrieve digital content)
gapNote: No app modelling saving, opening and organising digital work (files/folders) at infant level.
title: My Digital Drawer
concept: Children create a picture, name and save it, then find it again later; teaches the save-name-retrieve loop visually.

strand: Using technology safely and respectfully (online safety KS1)
gapNote: No KS1 e-safety tool on private information and where to go for help.
title: Keep It Private
concept: Sorting game: drag facts into Share or Keep Secret buckets, with instant gentle feedback and a who-to-tell signpost.

strand: Programming: sequence, selection, repetition and variables
gapNote: No app teaching loops, if-statements and variables in a Scratch-style environment for juniors.
title: Loop Lab
concept: Live Scratch-style canvas where dragging a repeat block visibly tightens a sprite's spiral; class tunes the count and condition.

strand: Designing, writing and debugging programs to solve problems
gapNote: Nothing supports decomposing a goal into a program and systematically debugging it.
title: Decompose-It
concept: Break a maze-solving challenge into sub-goals on sticky notes, then assemble them into working code with a step-through debugger.

strand: Computational thinking: decomposition, abstraction and logical reasoning
gapNote: No app explicitly drilling decomposition, pattern-spotting and abstraction as thinking skills.
title: Pattern Spotter
concept: Class finds the repeating rule in a sequence of tile patterns and generalises it into a single rule (abstraction made visible).

strand: Computer networks, the internet and the World Wide Web
gapNote: No app explaining networks, servers, routers and how a web page is requested and delivered.
title: Journey of a Web Request
concept: Animated packet travels from a pupil's device through router, ISP and server and back; class clicks each hop to learn its job.

strand: Data and information: collecting, analysing, evaluating and presenting
gapNote: No app on using spreadsheets/databases to collect, sort and present data and assess plausibility.
title: Data Detectives
concept: Class collects live class data into a mini-spreadsheet, sorts and charts it, then spots an implausible (faked) value.

strand: Online safety, digital literacy and identifying unacceptable behaviour
gapNote: No KS2 app on respectful use, recognising risk, and reporting content/contact concerns.
title: Spot the Red Flag
concept: Realistic chat/message scenarios; class flags risky messages and chooses report/block/tell, with reasoning feedback.

strand: Selecting and combining software for creative digital projects
gapNote: Nothing demonstrates combining tools (audio, image, text) to meet a brief on a range of devices.
title: Multimedia Maker
concept: Drag-and-drop project canvas where pupils combine image, sound and text layers to build a campaign poster against a brief.

strand: Computational thinking and algorithm design
gapNote: No app on designing, comparing and tracing algorithms (incl. searching and sorting) with abstraction.
title: Sort Race
concept: Animate bubble vs insertion sort side by side on the same data; class counts comparisons and predicts the winner.

strand: Programming in a text-based language (e.g. Python)
gapNote: No interactive Python sandbox covering sequence, selection, iteration, functions and data structures.
title: Python Trace Theatre
concept: Step a short Python program one line at a time while variable boxes update live; class predicts each next line.

strand: Data representation: binary, hexadecimal and storing media
gapNote: No app on binary/denary/hex conversion and how text, images and sound are stored as bits.
title: Binary Bulb Board
concept: Eight light bulbs as place values; toggle bits and the denary/hex totals update live, with a binary-image pixel mode.

strand: Computer systems: hardware, software and the CPU (fetch-decode-execute)
gapNote: No app modelling Von Neumann architecture, memory and the fetch-decode-execute cycle.
title: Inside the CPU
concept: Animated CPU where class clicks through fetch, decode and execute, watching the program counter and registers change.

strand: Networks, the internet and protocols
gapNote: No app on network topologies, packet switching, IP/DNS and how the web works at KS3 depth.
title: Packet Switch Simulator
concept: Split a message into packets and route them across a drawn network; nodes can fail so class sees re-routing in action.

strand: Boolean logic and logic circuits
gapNote: No app for AND/OR/NOT gates, building circuits and reading truth tables.
title: Logic Gate Workbench
concept: Wire AND/OR/NOT gates on a board; flip inputs and the auto-generated truth table and output bulb update instantly.

strand: Digital creativity and developing solutions to real problems
gapNote: Nothing supports designing/evaluating a multi-tool digital artefact against user needs.
title: Design-Build-Evaluate
concept: A guided studio: pupils plan a digital product to a brief, prototype it, then run a structured user-test and iterate.

strand: Online safety, digital resilience and responsible technology use
gapNote: No KS3 app on privacy, digital footprint, misinformation and respectful/legal online conduct.
title: Footprint Tracker
concept: Simulated profile where each post/like leaves a visible trail; class sees how much can be inferred and tightens settings.

strand: Systems architecture and memory (CPU, registers, primary/secondary storage)
gapNote: No app on CPU components, registers, the FDE cycle, RAM/ROM/virtual memory and storage types.
title: FDE Cycle Stepper
concept: Step the fetch-decode-execute cycle with live register, MAR/MDR and program counter values; class predicts each register's next value.

strand: Data representation (binary, hex, characters, images, sound, compression)
gapNote: No app on number-base conversion, character sets, sampling, resolution and compression methods.
title: Bits Behind the Image
concept: Drag resolution and colour-depth sliders on a photo; file size recalculates live so class sees the storage trade-off.

strand: Networks, topologies, protocols and the internet
gapNote: No app on LAN/WAN, the TCP/IP and OSI layers, protocols, DNS and packet switching at GCSE depth.
title: Protocol Stack Builder
concept: Drag a message down the TCP/IP layers, watching headers added at each layer, then strip them off at the receiver.

strand: Network and system security (threats and countermeasures)
gapNote: No app on malware, phishing, social engineering, encryption and prevention measures.
title: Caesar and Beyond
concept: Interactive cipher wheel for Caesar/substitution encryption; class encrypts, then cracks intercepted messages by frequency analysis.

strand: Algorithms (searching, sorting, computational thinking, trace tables)
gapNote: No app to visualise linear/binary search and bubble/merge/insertion sort and complete trace tables.
title: Search and Sort Visualiser
concept: Run binary vs linear search on the same array, with comparison counts and a live-filling trace table.

strand: Programming fundamentals and writing robust code
gapNote: No app on sequence/selection/iteration, data types, arrays, subprograms, file handling and validation.
title: Pseudocode to Python
concept: Side-by-side panes translate exam-board pseudocode into runnable Python; class fixes broken validation routines live.

strand: Boolean logic, data structures and databases (incl. SQL)
gapNote: No app on logic gates/truth tables, arrays/records and simple SQL queries on a database.
title: SQL Live Query
concept: Type SELECT/WHERE queries against a sample school database and watch the result table filter instantly.

strand: Impacts of digital technology (ethical, legal, environmental, privacy)
gapNote: No app on legislation (Data Protection, Computer Misuse, Copyright), ethics and environmental impact.
title: Tech Ethics Courtroom
concept: Class judges real-style scenarios against the relevant Act, voting on legality and seeing the law that applies.

strand: Programming paradigms and advanced techniques (OOP, recursion, functional)
gapNote: No app demonstrating object-oriented, recursive and functional programming techniques interactively.
title: Recursion Unrolled
concept: Visualise the call stack of a recursive function (e.g. factorial, Fibonacci) growing and unwinding frame by frame.

strand: Data structures and algorithms (trees, graphs, stacks, queues, Big-O)
gapNote: No app on abstract data types and algorithm complexity (Dijkstra, A*, traversals, sorting efficiency).
title: Graph Pathfinder
concept: Draw a weighted graph and watch Dijkstra/A* explore nodes step by step, with the priority queue shown alongside.

strand: Computer architecture, assembly and low-level operation
gapNote: No app on assembly instructions, addressing modes, pipelining and the detailed FDE cycle.
title: Little Man Computer
concept: Write and run assembly in an LMC-style model; class watches the accumulator, mailboxes and program counter change per instruction.

strand: Theory of computation (logic, finite state machines, Turing machines, regex)
gapNote: No app on FSMs, regular expressions, BNF, computability and the limits of computation.
title: FSM Builder
concept: Drag states and transitions to build a finite state machine, then feed it strings to see accept/reject live.

strand: Databases, big data and SQL (normalisation, transactions)
gapNote: No app on relational design, normalisation (1NF-3NF), ACID transactions and advanced SQL.
title: Normalise This
concept: Drag attributes between tables to take a flat dataset from 1NF to 3NF, with redundancy highlighted as it disappears.

strand: Networking, the internet and security (encryption, protocols, web tech)
gapNote: No app on TCP/IP layers in depth, client-server, encryption (symmetric/asymmetric) and web technologies.
title: Public Key Exchange
concept: Animate asymmetric encryption: class encrypts with a public key and watches only the private key decrypt, with tampering demos.

strand: Consequences and ethics of computing (legal, moral, cultural)
gapNote: No app on the ethical/legal/cultural impacts of computing, AI bias and emerging technologies.
title: Algorithm on Trial
concept: Class interrogates a biased hiring/decision algorithm, adjusts its data and rules, and debates accountability.

strand: Systematic software development (analysis, design, testing, maintenance)
gapNote: No app on the development lifecycle, methodologies, testing strategies and project documentation.
title: Lifecycle Simulator
concept: Steer a project through analysis-design-build-test-maintain; injected requirement changes show why iteration matters.

strand: Exploring and using materials, tools and techniques (Creating with Materials)
gapNote: No app lets young children explore joining, shaping and constructing with everyday materials and simple tools.
title: Junk Model Maker
concept: Drag-and-drop a giant on-screen box of recycled materials (boxes, tubes, lids) and choose how to join them (tape, glue, slot) to build a model the class designs together.

strand: Constructing and building structures (blocks, kits, joining)
gapNote: No app supports building and testing simple structures or talking about why a tower falls.
title: Build It Up
concept: Stack and arrange on-screen blocks to make a tower or bridge, then press a wobble button to test if it stays up and discuss why wide bases are stronger.

strand: Designing and making for a purpose (planning, talking about what to make)
gapNote: No app prompts children to say what they will make, who it is for and what it needs to do.
title: What Shall We Make?
concept: A picture-led planner where the class picks a purpose (a den for teddy, a boat that floats) and chooses materials, then compares the plan with the finished photo.

strand: Food, cooking and healthy eating in the early years
gapNote: No app introduces simple food preparation, hygiene and where food comes from for early years.
title: Wash, Chop, Mix
concept: An interactive kitchen where children tap the steps in order (wash hands, wash fruit, mix) to make a fruit salad, with hygiene reminders that pop up.

strand: Design (purposeful, functional, appealing products for self and others; communicating ideas)
gapNote: No app guides pupils to generate ideas and communicate a design for a chosen user and purpose.
title: Design for a User
concept: Class chooses a user (a baby, a postman) and a need, then drags features onto a product template and labels the design, talking through why each choice suits the user.

strand: Make (selecting tools and materials; cutting, shaping, joining and finishing)
gapNote: No app models choosing tools and the cut/shape/join/finish sequence at KS1.
title: Choose Your Tools
concept: Given a making job, pupils drag the right tools and materials from a tray, then order the make into cut, shape, join, finish steps and watch it assemble.

strand: Evaluate (exploring existing products and judging their own against design criteria)
gapNote: No app supports product evaluation against simple agreed criteria.
title: Good Design Detective
concept: Project a real product (a lunchbox, a coat) and let the class tick or cross it against criteria like easy to open, strong, fun, then judge their own make the same way.

strand: Technical knowledge: simple structures (making structures stronger, stiffer, more stable)
gapNote: No app demonstrates strengthening and stabilising simple structures.
title: Strong, Stiff, Stable
concept: Pupils fold, layer or brace an on-screen paper structure and a load-tester shows how each change lets it hold more weight.

strand: Technical knowledge: simple mechanisms (levers, sliders, wheels and axles)
gapNote: No app lets pupils manipulate basic mechanisms to make things move.
title: Make It Move
concept: Drag a slider or lever onto a moving picture (a peeping animal, a waving hand) and see the mechanism animate, exploring pivots and how far things move.

strand: Cooking and nutrition (where food comes from; basic food preparation and hygiene)
gapNote: No app teaches food origins and simple, safe preparation at KS1.
title: Farm to Plate
concept: Trace ingredients back to plant or animal sources with a tap-to-reveal map, then assemble a simple no-cook snack following hygiene steps.

strand: Design (research, design criteria, communicating via sketches, cross-sections, exploded diagrams and CAD)
gapNote: No app supports researching users and communicating designs through annotated sketches, diagrams or CAD at KS2.
title: Design Brief Builder
concept: Class researches a real user need, writes a design brief and specification, then drafts annotated sketches and a simple exploded diagram on a shared canvas.

strand: Make (precise tool use; range of materials and components; accurate measuring, marking, cutting)
gapNote: No app models accurate measuring, marking out and component selection for KS2 making.
title: Mark It Out
concept: Pupils measure, mark and cut a virtual material to a tolerance, with a ruler and try-square overlay that flags inaccurate cuts before they commit.

strand: Evaluate (investigating key events and individuals in D&T; evaluating against criteria and improving)
gapNote: No app covers product investigation, design history or iterative improvement at KS2.
title: Iterate and Improve
concept: Test a prototype against the spec, log what failed, then adjust the design and re-test, building a visible improvement timeline.

strand: Technical knowledge: structures (frame structures, reinforcing, triangulation)
gapNote: No app demonstrates stronger frame structures and triangulation at KS2.
title: Bridge Load Lab
concept: Build a frame bridge, add triangulation and bracing, then drive increasing loads across it and watch where it bends or breaks.

strand: Technical knowledge: mechanical systems (gears, pulleys, cams, levers, linkages)
gapNote: No D&T app explores gears, pulleys, cams and linkages to create movement; the science circuit app does not cover mechanics.
title: Gear and Pulley Playground
concept: Connect gears and pulleys of different sizes and watch live ratios change speed and direction, then design a mechanism for a given job.

strand: Technical knowledge: electrical systems (simple circuits, switches, bulbs, buzzers, motors)
gapNote: No D&T-purposed app embeds circuits into a product context; science-circuit-builder.html teaches circuits as physics, not as a D&T system in a product.
title: Power Your Product
concept: Wire a switch, bulb, buzzer or motor into a product the class designs (a torch, a alarm), testing the circuit then placing it inside the product.

strand: Cooking and nutrition (principles of a healthy and varied diet; preparing dishes; seasonality and food sourcing)
gapNote: No app teaches healthy balanced diets, savoury dish preparation and food provenance at KS2.
title: Balance the Plate
concept: Drag foods onto an Eatwell-style plate and get live feedback on balance, then plan and sequence a simple savoury dish using a range of techniques.

strand: Design (user-centred design, briefs and specifications, exploring contexts, communicating with sketching, modelling and CAD)
gapNote: No app supports KS3 user-centred briefs, specifications and communication of design intent.
title: From Context to Spec
concept: Pupils interrogate a design context, identify user needs, write a measurable specification, then sketch and annotate concepts that meet each spec point.

strand: Make (planning, selecting from wider materials and components, precise tools, techniques and processes)
gapNote: No app models KS3 production planning, material selection and accurate manufacturing techniques.
title: Production Plan Simulator
concept: Sequence the manufacture of a product, allocate tools, materials and quality checks, then run it and see where bottlenecks or errors occur.

strand: Evaluate (testing and critiquing against specification; investigating past and present design and technology)
gapNote: No app supports structured KS3 evaluation, testing and analysis of existing products.
title: Critique the Product
concept: Dissect a real product on screen, evaluate it against a spec, ergonomics and sustainability, then propose evidence-based improvements.

strand: Technical knowledge: materials and their properties (timbers, metals, polymers, textiles, smart and modern materials)
gapNote: No app teaches categories of materials, their working properties and selection for purpose.
title: Material Selector
concept: Pick a product requirement and compare timbers, metals, polymers and smart materials on properties like strength, density and cost to justify a choice.

strand: Technical knowledge: mechanical and electronic systems (mechanisms, inputs-process-outputs, programmable components)
gapNote: No app links mechanical and electronic systems with inputs, processes and outputs at KS3; science apps do not frame this as systems design.
title: Systems Block Builder
concept: Wire input, process and output blocks (sensor to microcontroller to motor or LED) into a working system simulation and tweak the logic to change behaviour.

strand: Cooking and nutrition (cook a repertoire of savoury dishes; understand source, seasonality and characteristics of ingredients; healthy diet)
gapNote: No app supports KS3 nutrition, dish development and ingredient knowledge.
title: Nutrient Lab
concept: Build a meal and watch macro and micronutrient totals update live against dietary guidelines, then adapt the recipe to hit a nutritional target.

strand: Core technical principles (materials and their properties, energy generation and storage, modern and smart materials, systems)
gapNote: No app covers GCSE core technical principles across material categories and energy.
title: Smart Material Showcase
concept: Interact with shape memory alloys, thermochromics and composites by changing temperature, stress or current and seeing the material respond, then match each to a product use.

strand: Specialist technical principles (forces, stresses, structural reinforcement, mechanical devices, electronic systems and programming)
gapNote: No app models GCSE specialist principles such as forces, mechanical devices or electronic system design.
title: Stress and Reinforcement Lab
concept: Apply tension, compression, shear and torsion to a component and trial reinforcement methods (webbing, lamination, triangulation) to resist failure.

strand: Designing and making principles (iterative design, user-centred design, ergonomics, prototyping, tolerances, quality control)
gapNote: No app supports the GCSE iterative design and prototyping process or tolerances and QC.
title: Iterative Design Loop
concept: Run a full design cycle on screen: explore, create, evaluate, refine, with version history and user feedback feeding each iteration.

strand: Industry, manufacture and sustainability (scales of production, CAD/CAM, lifecycle, the 6 Rs, social and environmental impact)
gapNote: No app addresses manufacturing scales, CAD/CAM or sustainability and the 6 Rs.
title: Production Scale Simulator
concept: Choose one-off, batch or mass production for a product and compare cost, time, tooling and CAD/CAM use, then assess the lifecycle and 6 Rs impact.

strand: Food preparation and cooking skills (techniques, the science of cooking, food safety)
gapNote: No app covers GCSE Food Prep cooking techniques or the science of heat transfer and functional properties.
title: Cooking Science Bench
concept: Manipulate heat, time and ingredients to see denaturation, gelatinisation, aeration and caramelisation happen, then explain each functional change.

strand: Nutrition, diet and food provenance (nutrients, energy needs, diet-related health, food choice, sourcing and processing)
gapNote: No app supports GCSE nutrition science, diet-related health or food provenance.
title: Diet Profiler
concept: Build a day of meals for a chosen profile (athlete, toddler, diabetic) and analyse energy and nutrient balance against requirements, then adjust to meet needs.

strand: Materials, components and systems (advanced material properties, selection, smart/modern materials, electronic and mechanical systems)
gapNote: No app covers A-level depth on advanced materials selection and systems.
title: Advanced Material Matrix
concept: Compare advanced materials and composites on a multi-axis property chart, run a weighted selection matrix for a brief and justify the optimal choice.

strand: Performance characteristics, forces and structural analysis (stress, strain, factors of safety, mechanical advantage)
gapNote: No app supports quantitative structural and mechanical analysis at A-level.
title: Stress-Strain Studio
concept: Load virtual specimens, plot live stress-strain curves, read off yield and ultimate strength and calculate factors of safety for a design decision.

strand: Design theory, ergonomics and human factors (anthropometrics, percentiles, inclusive and user-centred design)
gapNote: No app teaches anthropometric data, percentiles and inclusive design at A-level.
title: Anthropometrics Explorer
concept: Set a target percentile range and watch product dimensions auto-adjust to fit, testing inclusive design against real anthropometric datasets.

strand: Manufacturing, processes and CAD/CAM (industrial processes, automation, scales of production, digital manufacture)
gapNote: No app covers advanced manufacturing processes, automation and CAD/CAM at A-level.
title: Process Chooser Pro
concept: Match a component to the right industrial process (injection moulding, CNC, additive) by trialling each on cost, volume, tolerance and finish, then justify the choice.

strand: Sustainability, lifecycle and wider impacts (lifecycle analysis, circular economy, social, ethical, legal and environmental responsibility)
gapNote: No app supports A-level lifecycle analysis, circular economy or wider responsibilities.
title: Lifecycle Analyst
concept: Run a full lifecycle assessment of a product from extraction to disposal, model circular-economy interventions and see the carbon and impact totals shift.

strand: Design and make project / NEA process (briefs, research, iterative development, testing, evaluation and commercial viability)
gapNote: No app scaffolds the A-level NEA iterative design and evaluation process.
title: NEA Design Journey
concept: A structured project workspace that guides exploration, client research, iterative prototyping, testing and a commercial-viability evaluation with evidence capture at each stage.

strand: Gross motor skills (moving, balancing, coordination)
gapNote: No app supports fundamental movement, balance or spatial awareness for Reception.
title: Movement Mat
concept: Projected animated movement cards (hop, skip, balance, crawl) the class follows in real time, with a teacher dial to sequence a mini circuit.

strand: Fine motor skills and hand control
gapNote: Nothing covers grip, manipulation or tool/equipment control linked to physical play.
title: Grip and Grab
concept: Touchscreen drag tasks (threading, pinching, tracing) projected for modelling correct pencil and tool grip before active sessions.

strand: Healthy bodies and active play
gapNote: No app introduces being active, why bodies feel different after movement, or healthy routines.
title: My Busy Body
concept: Interactive avatar whose heartbeat and breathing speed up as the class does actions, prompting talk about what exercise does to us.

strand: Fundamental movement skills (run, jump, throw, catch)
gapNote: No app models or assesses core locomotor and object-control skills.
title: Throw and Catch Coach
concept: Slow-motion technique animation with a tap-to-pause breakdown of the throw/catch action, plus a class self-assessment thumbs grid.

strand: Balance, agility and coordination
gapNote: Nothing supports developing or demonstrating balance and coordination.
title: Agility Ladder Lab
concept: Animated footwork ladder patterns the teacher projects and speeds up, with arrows showing where feet go for each agility drill.

strand: Team games: simple attacking and defending
gapNote: No app introduces basic tactics, space and rules of simple games.
title: Space Invaders PE
concept: Top-down pitch where children drag attacker/defender counters to discover finding space and marking, with a teacher reveal of best moves.

strand: Dance and movement to music
gapNote: No app supports performing simple movement patterns or rhythm work.
title: Move to the Beat
concept: Beat-synced movement prompts on the board (stretch, turn, jump) building a class dance phrase the children remember and perform.

strand: Healthy, active lifestyles
gapNote: Nothing connects movement to health, warm-ups or how the body responds to exercise at KS1.
title: Warm-Up Wizard
concept: Animated character leading a pulse-raiser/stretch routine with a slider the class uses to feel heart-rate change before and after.

strand: Invasion games: tactics, attacking and defending
gapNote: No app teaches positioning, space and tactics for football, hockey, netball or basketball.
title: Tactics Board Live
concept: Drag-and-drop pitch for netball/football where pupils plan an attack, then the app animates the play to test if it works.

strand: Net/wall and striking/fielding games
gapNote: Nothing covers tennis, badminton, cricket or rounders skills, rules or fielding tactics.
title: Field the Ball
concept: Striking/fielding diamond where pupils position fielders against different hits and see the percentage of balls stopped.

strand: Gymnastics: sequences, flexibility and strength
gapNote: No app supports building and refining movement sequences or apparatus work.
title: Sequence Builder
concept: Drag gymnastics shapes/rolls/jumps into a timeline to compose a balanced routine, with auto feedback on variety and flow.

strand: Dance: complex patterns and composition
gapNote: Nothing supports choreographing or evaluating more complex dance.
title: Choreo Canvas
concept: Timeline tool to arrange motifs, levels and formations, previewing the routine with stick-figure dancers for class critique.

strand: Athletics: running, jumping and throwing for performance
gapNote: No app develops or measures sprint, distance, jump or throw technique and progress.
title: Athletics Arena
concept: Technique animations plus a class results board pupils enter times/distances into, auto-charting personal bests.

strand: Outdoor and adventurous activity (problem solving)
gapNote: No app supports orienteering, teamwork challenges or problem solving.
title: Orienteering Quest
concept: Projected map with control points where teams plan the fastest route and the app times decisions and reveals the optimal path.

strand: Swimming and water safety (statutory 25m, strokes, self-rescue)
gapNote: Nothing supports stroke technique, water safety or tracking the 25m statutory outcome.
title: Stroke Lab
concept: Side-on animations of front crawl/breaststroke with a fault-finder where pupils spot and fix technique errors.

strand: Evaluating performance and improvement
gapNote: No app supports comparing own/others performance to personal bests and setting next steps.
title: Coach's Eye
concept: Side-by-side clips/diagrams pupils annotate with strengths and one improvement, building peer-assessment habits.

strand: Competitive games: advanced tactics and strategy
gapNote: No app develops decision-making, formations or game strategy across invasion/net/striking games.
title: Game Plan Studio
concept: Interactive formation board where students set up a team system, then run animated scenarios to test the tactic against an opponent.

strand: Refining techniques and skilled performance
gapNote: Nothing supports analysing and refining technique across a range of activities.
title: Technique Analyser
concept: Phase-by-phase models of skills (serve, layup, vault) with a tap-to-mark checklist the class uses to coach a volunteer.

strand: Dance and gymnastics: advanced composition
gapNote: No app supports more demanding choreography, apparatus sequences or performance evaluation.
title: Composition Lab
concept: Drag motifs, canon, unison and dynamics onto a timeline and preview the staged piece for whole-class refinement.

strand: Outdoor and adventurous activities (teamwork, problem solving)
gapNote: Nothing supports OAA leadership, navigation or group problem solving at KS3.
title: Expedition Planner
concept: Teams plan a route under time/weather/equipment constraints; the app simulates consequences of their choices.

strand: Fitness, health and active lifestyles
gapNote: No app covers components of fitness, training types or healthy lifestyle behaviours.
title: Fitness Components Lab
concept: Interactive matcher linking sports to fitness components, then build a circuit and see which components it trains.

strand: Analysing and evaluating performance
gapNote: No app supports structured analysis to identify strengths and improvement priorities.
title: Performance Profiler
concept: Radar-chart tool where students rate a performance on key criteria and the app prioritises one development target.

strand: Applied anatomy and physiology
gapNote: No app covers musculoskeletal, cardiorespiratory and energy systems for GCSE PE.
title: Body in Motion
concept: Clickable muscle and skeleton model showing antagonistic pairs and joint actions when students animate a chosen movement.

strand: Movement analysis (levers, planes and axes)
gapNote: Nothing supports lever systems, planes/axes or mechanical advantage analysis.
title: Lever Lab
concept: Drag the fulcrum, load and effort on a limb diagram to classify lever systems and compute mechanical advantage live.

strand: Physical training and fitness testing
gapNote: No app covers principles of training, methods or fitness test protocols and data.
title: Training Programme Designer
concept: Build a periodised plan applying SPORT/FITT, with the app flagging overtraining and progression errors.

strand: Sport psychology
gapNote: Nothing covers skill classification, goal setting, arousal or guidance/feedback.
title: Arousal and Performance
concept: Drag the inverted-U curve for different skills/athletes to predict optimal arousal, with worked exam-style scenarios.

strand: Socio-cultural influences in sport
gapNote: No app covers participation, commercialisation, ethics or engagement patterns.
title: Sport and Society Explorer
concept: Interactive data dashboard on participation by group, with toggles linking barriers to strategies that raise engagement.

strand: Health, fitness and well-being
gapNote: Nothing covers diet, energy balance, sedentary lifestyle consequences for GCSE.
title: Energy Balance Simulator
concept: Adjust diet and activity inputs to model energy balance and long-term health outcomes, with macronutrient breakdown.

strand: Practical performance and personal exercise programme
gapNote: No app supports practical assessment criteria or the coursework PEP analysis cycle.
title: PEP Builder
concept: Guided coursework scaffold that plans, logs and analyses a personal exercise programme with auto-generated progress graphs.

strand: Exercise physiology and energy systems
gapNote: No app covers ATP-PC/glycolytic/aerobic systems, oxygen uptake or recovery at A-level depth.
title: Energy Systems Continuum
concept: Adjust intensity and duration to see which energy system dominates, animating fuel use, fatigue and EPOC recovery.

strand: Biomechanics
gapNote: Nothing covers force, motion, projectile, angular motion or fluid mechanics.
title: Biomechanics Sandbox
concept: Launch a projectile and vary angle, speed and height while the app plots trajectory and explains the governing factors.

strand: Skill acquisition and motor learning
gapNote: No app covers learning theories, practice/feedback types or information processing models.
title: Information Processing Lab
concept: Interactive Whiting/Welford model where students route a sporting example through each stage and test reaction-time effects.

strand: Sport psychology (advanced)
gapNote: Nothing covers attribution, leadership, group dynamics or stress management at A-level.
title: Attribution Modeller
concept: Place outcomes on the Weiner locus/stability grid and see predicted effects on confidence and motivation.

strand: Sport and society and contemporary issues
gapNote: No app covers the development of sport, globalisation, commercialisation or technology.
title: Sport Through Time
concept: Interactive timeline from pre-industrial to global sport, toggling social factors to show how participation evolved.

strand: Performance analysis and improvement (coursework/EAPI)
gapNote: Nothing supports the analytical evaluation and programme of improvement coursework cycle.
title: EAPI Studio
concept: Structured analysis tool to identify a performance weakness, justify causes against theory and build an improvement programme.

strand: Rules, fairness and taking part (classroom and community)
gapNote: No app on why we have rules, fairness, turn-taking or class decision-making for infants.
title: Build Our Class Rules
concept: Class drags suggested rules onto a board, votes with a live tally, and sees instantly how a 'no rule' classroom descends into chaos via an animated playground simulation.

strand: Belonging: me, my family and my community
gapNote: Nothing on identity, families and the people who help us in the local community.
title: Who Helps Us?
concept: An interactive village map where tapping a building (school, surgery, fire station) reveals the helper, what they do, and a 'thank you' action the class designs.

strand: Looking after our world (caring for places and living things)
gapNote: No app on responsibility for shared spaces, litter, recycling or caring for animals/plants.
title: Tidy Town
concept: A drag-and-sort recycling game on a shared town scene where the class collectively cleans a park and watches a live 'happiness meter' rise.

strand: Money: what it is and choices we make
gapNote: No early-finance app on coins, needs vs wants or simple spending choices.
title: Needs or Wants?
concept: Items fly across the screen and the class sorts them into needs vs wants, then spends a fixed pot of coins in a shop with instant change feedback.

strand: Right and wrong: simple choices and consequences
gapNote: No app modelling simple moral choices and their consequences for infants.
title: What Happens Next?
concept: Branching animated stories where the class votes on a character's choice and watches the consequence play out, then discusses a kinder option.

strand: Democracy, voting and how decisions are made
gapNote: No app introducing voting, school councils or how group decisions are made democratically.
title: Run a School Election
concept: Pupils create manifesto cards, the class casts secret ballots, and a live results board shows first-past-the-post in action with a turnout counter.

strand: Rules, laws and why society needs them
gapNote: No app distinguishing rules from laws or exploring why laws exist and who makes them.
title: Rule or Law?
concept: A live sorter where pupils classify statements as a school rule, a law, or neither, then see the consequence ladder for breaking each.

strand: Communities, diversity and identity in the UK
gapNote: No app exploring the diverse communities, cultures and identities that make up modern Britain.
title: Our Diverse Community
concept: An interactive 'town' the class populates with people of different backgrounds, faiths and jobs, revealing how diversity strengthens a community via mini-stories.

strand: Money management and being a sensible consumer
gapNote: No app on budgeting pocket money, saving, value for money or being a careful consumer.
title: Budget the Class Trip
concept: The class plans a day out within a fixed budget, dragging activities and snacks into a live-totalling planner that flags overspend.

strand: Active citizenship: making a difference locally
gapNote: No app on identifying a local issue and planning a campaign or social action project.
title: Campaign Builder
concept: Pupils pick a local issue, then assemble a campaign (poster, petition, who to write to) on a planning board that simulates how much impact each action adds.

strand: UK government, Parliament and the democratic system
gapNote: No app on the structure of UK government, Parliament, the role of the monarch, the PM and Cabinet.
title: How Parliament Works
concept: An interactive Westminster diagram where students move a bill through Commons, Lords and Royal Assent, hitting amendments and votes at each live stage.

strand: Elections, voting systems and political participation
gapNote: No app on how elections work, first-past-the-post vs other systems, or ways citizens participate.
title: Election Lab
concept: Students run the same vote through FPTP, AV and PR side by side and watch how the same ballots produce different winners and seat shares.

strand: The law, justice system and how laws are made and enforced
gapNote: No app on the legal system, courts, civil vs criminal law, the police or the justice process.
title: Courtroom Pathways
concept: An interactive flowchart taking a case from arrest through charge, court and sentence, where students decide civil vs criminal and see where each route leads.

strand: Local and devolved government and the role of the citizen
gapNote: No app on local councils, devolved administrations, public services and how citizens engage.
title: Run the Council
concept: Students allocate a council budget across services, then face the consequences (potholes, school cuts, public reaction) in a live what-if dashboard.

strand: Personal finance: money, budgeting, credit and risk
gapNote: No app on income, budgeting, saving, borrowing, interest, debt risk and financial planning at KS3.
title: Money Months
concept: Students manage a young person's monthly budget through random life events, watching savings, debt and interest compound across a year.

strand: The UK's role in the wider world and global issues
gapNote: No app on the UK's relationship with Europe, the Commonwealth, the UN or global citizenship.
title: Global Connections Map
concept: An interactive world map where students explore the UK's membership of the UN, NATO and Commonwealth and trace how a global issue reaches local lives.

strand: Identity, diversity and living together in the UK
gapNote: No app on the diverse national, regional, religious and ethnic identities of the UK and mutual respect.
title: What Makes Us British?
concept: A live word-cloud and sorting activity where students build a shared picture of identity and diversity, testing assumptions against real census data.

strand: Parliamentary democracy and the British political system
gapNote: No app on the key elements of the constitution, power, the executive, legislature and judiciary at KS4.
title: Power Map of Britain
concept: An interactive constitution diagram where students trace how power flows and is checked between Crown, Parliament, government and courts, stress-tested with real scenarios.

strand: Elections, political parties and citizen participation
gapNote: No app on parties, manifestos, pressure groups, campaigning and active participation at GCSE level.
title: Win the Marginal
concept: Students run a campaign in a marginal seat, spending limited resources on tactics and watching live polling shift in response.

strand: The legal system, justice and the rights of citizens
gapNote: No app on the operation of justice, courts, legal rights, employment and consumer law at KS4.
title: Know Your Rights
concept: An interactive case-tree on consumer, employment and police-encounter rights where students choose actions and see the legal outcome and remedy.

strand: Active citizenship and social action projects
gapNote: No app to plan, run and evaluate a citizenship social action campaign (a core GCSE requirement).
title: Action Project Planner
concept: A live project board guiding students from issue to advocacy method to measurable impact, modelling stakeholder reactions as they choose tactics.

strand: Personal finance, taxation and public money
gapNote: No app on income tax, NI, VAT, the public purse, where tax goes and personal financial planning at KS4.
title: Where Your Tax Goes
concept: Students set tax rates and a national budget and watch services, deficit and public reaction respond live, linking the payslip to public spending.

strand: The media, public debate and being a critical citizen
gapNote: No app on the role of a free press, misinformation, bias and evaluating sources as a citizen.
title: Spot the Spin
concept: Students compare how outlets report the same event, tagging bias, framing and missing context, with a live 'reliability score' they justify.

strand: The UK, Europe and the international community
gapNote: No app on the UK's post-Brexit relationship with Europe, the UN, NATO and international law at KS4.
title: Britain in the World
concept: An interactive treaty-and-membership board where students manage the UK's international ties and see trade, security and influence trade-offs.

strand: Politics, power and democratic theory
gapNote: No app on democratic models, ideologies, sovereignty and the distribution of power for post-16 study.
title: Ideology Compass
concept: Students place real policies on a political-spectrum grid and see how liberal, conservative and socialist traditions would each respond, with live debate prompts.

strand: Citizen participation, campaigning and advocacy
gapNote: No app on advanced advocacy, lobbying, direct action and measuring civic impact at KS5.
title: Pressure Group Strategist
concept: Students run a pressure-group campaign choosing insider vs outsider tactics and watch influence, media coverage and policy change respond in real time.

strand: Law, rights and global justice
gapNote: No app on human rights frameworks, international law and global justice issues for post-16 learners.
title: Rights on Trial
concept: Students argue landmark human-rights cases, weighing competing rights and seeing how courts balanced them, with a live verdict-prediction model.

strand: Financial capability for adult life
gapNote: No app on student finance, tax, pensions, mortgages, credit scores and major life financial decisions for 16-19.
title: Adult Life Finance Simulator
concept: Students steer a young adult through university, first job, renting and a mortgage, watching credit score, debt and net worth evolve across a decade.

strand: Global citizenship and contemporary world issues
gapNote: No app on sustainability, migration, conflict and the citizen's role in global challenges at KS5.
title: Global Challenge Room
concept: Teams negotiate a global problem (climate, migration) as competing nations, seeing how national interest and cooperation reshape the outcome live.
